svg.title: Plato — Early life
svg.desc: Minimal card with left accent bar and biographical text.

div.p: Plato was born into an aristocratic Athenian milieu during the Peloponnesian War era. Biographical detail is partly legendary, but the standard story stresses an early interest in politics, shattered by Socrates' trial and execution (399 BCE)—an event Plato portrayed as a civic and moral crisis. Later traditions describe travels (often cited: Italy/Sicily, sometimes Egypt) that influenced his views on governance and mathematics. He returned to Athens and founded the Academy around 387 BCE, creating an enduring institutional center for mathematics and philosophy.
